caveats: a: The occupational proxy is broader than both the six-digit code and the narrowed lens.
a: The sub-lens itself mixes equipment-build and consumable-element intensity.
rho: Targets with poor serial-number and configuration histories will realize less value.
rho: AI output affecting process safety, sanitation, or equipment balance requires accountable review.
e: No public dataset isolates the narrowed sub-lens from the residual code.
e: The provided LMM firm-count input is missing and is not replaced here.
s5: Broad manufacturing transactions include many unlike business models.
s5: The missing dataset firm count prevents converting this rate into an absolute opportunity.
q: Public pages demonstrate the revenue architecture, not contract economics.
q: The range depends heavily on proprietary installed-base share versus cross-compatible consumables.
d5: Broad industrial production is not a product-specific forecast.
d5: EPA financing is an opportunity set, not a guaranteed equipment order pipeline.
o: Some technical support and parts-ordering volume can become self-service.
o: Demand elimination from process redesign is captured mainly in d5 to avoid double counting.

lens.customer_and_revenue_model: The coherent sub-lens sells engineered capital equipment through quotes and applications work, followed by recurring replacement screens, filter elements, wear parts, maintenance, field service, rebuilds, and emergency support over a long installed-base life.
lens.deviation_from_default: NAICS 333998 is a residual code spanning unrelated products including fire-sprinkler systems, balers, bridge lifts, centrifuges, cremation ovens, sieves, jacks, industrial filters, and scales. One commercial and demand screen would be incoherent, so the lens is narrowed to industrial separation, screening, centrifuge, and filtration equipment with common process-industry customers and recurring aftermarket workflows; the narrowing is for business-model coherence, not attractiveness.
lens.excluded_activities: Automatic fire-sprinkler systems, balers, bridge and gate lifting machinery, cremating ovens, hydraulic or pneumatic jacks, scales and balances, makers without recurring external-customer revenue, captive units, and platform-scale manufacturers outside the lower middle market.
lens.included_activities: U.S. lower-middle-market manufacturers of industrial separation, screening, sifting, centrifuge, and filtration machinery that provide repeat aftermarket parts, replacement elements or screens, rebuilds, or ongoing service to external industrial and municipal customers.

a.bridge: BLS occupation data cover all three-digit machinery manufacturing rather than the narrowed 333998 sub-lens. I map office/administrative, engineering, management, business/financial, sales, computer, application support, quotation, planning, and documentation tasks into partial AI exposure, while treating fabrication, assembly, balancing, installation, field repair, and testing as mostly physical. Engineered separation-equipment firms may employ more applications and aftermarket staff than broad machinery, so the proxy could understate exposure; filter-element manufacturers may make it overstate exposure.
caveats: The occupational proxy is broader than both the six-digit code and the narrowed lens.
The sub-lens itself mixes equipment-build and consumable-element intensity.
a.change_evidence: A payroll-weighted occupation and task survey covering U.S. LMM screening, centrifuge, and industrial-filtration manufacturers would materially narrow the range.
a.rationale: Product selection, quotation, prior-design retrieval, applications engineering, spare-parts matching, technical support, field-service reporting, purchasing, planning, and administration offer substantial task-level exposure. Physical manufacturing, test, rebuild, and site work keep the majority of labor operator-bound.

d5.bridge: Federal Reserve industrial production measures the broad U.S. industrial customer base, and EPA programs support water infrastructure generally rather than demand for the narrowed machinery basket. I combine the diversified food, pharmaceutical, chemical, mining, plastics, wastewater, industrial, and municipal end markets documented by manufacturers with ongoing water-infrastructure finance and modest current industrial growth to infer slight five-year real demand growth. The bridge could overstate growth if capital spending weakens or installed equipment lasts longer than expected.
caveats: Broad industrial production is not a product-specific forecast.
EPA financing is an opportunity set, not a guaranteed equipment order pipeline.
d5.change_evidence: Five-year U.S. shipment forecasts for industrial screening, centrifuges, and filtration equipment and consumables, weighted by each candidate's end-market and aftermarket mix, would materially change the range.
d5.rationale: Process separation and filtration are recurring physical requirements, while replacement screens, elements, wear parts, and rebuilds follow installed equipment usage. Water infrastructure and regulated process quality support demand; cyclical capital budgets, long equipment lives, customer consolidation, and cheaper imports constrain it.

caveats: No public dataset isolates the narrowed sub-lens from the residual code.
The provided LMM firm-count input is missing and is not replaced here.
e.change_evidence: A firm-level 333998 product classification matched to revenue by original equipment, aftermarket consumables, parts, and service, plus ownership and EBITDA, would materially change the estimate.
e.rationale: The narrowed product families commonly support repeat replacement parts, screens, elements, rebuilds, and field service, making firms within them eligible. The residual NAICS code also contains unrelated product categories, firms without an installed-base aftermarket, corporate establishments, and companies outside the LMM band, so only a bounded judgment is defensible.

l.detail: BLS Industry Productivity (IPS) at ancestor 3339: labor compensation (L02) $32,860.2M / sectoral output (T30) $118,353.9M, year 2024

n.detail: margin 0.1962 (margins.json[33]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Machinery EBITDA/Sales 19.62%); revenue/employee $374,276 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 33399); DATA GAP: no SUSB size-class data for this code; n_band could not be computed — the research run must supply it with a shown chain, quality ESTIMATE
n.rationale: dataset n_band has no defensible value

caveats: Some technical support and parts-ordering volume can become self-service.
Demand elimination from process redesign is captured mainly in d5 to avoid double counting.
o.change_evidence: Customer evidence on in-house fabrication, automated equipment configuration, predictive maintenance that eliminates service visits, and process redesign that removes separation or filtration steps would materially change the estimate.
o.rationale: Customers still require physical screening, separation, centrifuge, and filtration equipment, replacement media or screens, machining, balancing, installation, and repair. AI can enable self-service selection and troubleshooting but cannot supply the physical process function entirely as software.

q.bridge: Manufacturer and specialist pages document recurring aftermarket parts, replacement screens and filters, repairs, rebuilds, emergency response, and long-lived installed equipment, but do not disclose retained productivity benefit. I infer above-midpoint retention where compatibility, uptime, process knowledge, and rapid delivery matter, then discount for competitive cross-references, customer procurement, and eventual contract repricing. This proxy may overstate retention for standardized replacement elements with transparent alternatives.
caveats: Public pages demonstrate the revenue architecture, not contract economics.
The range depends heavily on proprietary installed-base share versus cross-compatible consumables.
q.change_evidence: Gross-margin and price realization by original equipment, proprietary aftermarket, cross-reference consumables, rebuild, and field service following measured workflow savings would materially change the estimate.
q.rationale: Installed-base knowledge and downtime avoidance support pricing power in proprietary parts, emergency repairs, rebuilds, and engineered replacements. Standardized filter elements and screens are more contestable, and sophisticated plants can share savings during rebids.

caveats: Targets with poor serial-number and configuration histories will realize less value.
AI output affecting process safety, sanitation, or equipment balance requires accountable review.
rho.change_evidence: Before-and-after operating data from comparable firms on quote cycle time, parts identification, service-report time, first-time fix rate, and applications-engineering hours would materially change the estimate.
rho.rationale: Within five years, an operator can plausibly implement retrieval and drafting tools for quotes, applications, parts cross-references, troubleshooting, manuals, inspection reports, scheduling, and procurement. Realization is constrained by fragmented legacy drawings, inconsistent installed-base records, variable process materials, regulatory requirements, and the need for engineering or technician review.

s5.bridge: BizBuySell closed transactions cover broad manufacturing, while Census owner-characteristics tables cover businesses across the economy; neither measures control transfers among eligible 333998 separation-equipment firms. I use them to infer a moderate five-year succession and sale probability, with a downward adjustment for technical, export-exposed, or corporate-owned manufacturers. Broad small-business listings may overstate qualifying control transfers in this specialized sub-lens.
caveats: Broad manufacturing transactions include many unlike business models.
The missing dataset firm count prevents converting this rate into an absolute opportunity.
s5.change_evidence: A proprietary ownership map with owner age, corporate affiliation, prior recapitalizations, and closed transactions for U.S. screening, centrifuge, and industrial-filtration manufacturers would materially change the estimate.
s5.rationale: Manufacturing businesses exhibit transaction flow and aging owners create succession pressure, but specialized machinery businesses require technical continuity and attract a narrower buyer set. The probability can be bounded even though the absolute target count cannot.

principal_driver: A long-lived installed base generates repeat parts, screens, filters, rebuild, and service workflows whose historical configuration data can compound AI-enabled productivity.
principal_weakness: The residual classification and missing LMM firm count prevent a defensible estimate of the absolute transferable target universe.

run_meta.title: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing

sources.supports: Defines the residual industry and lists disparate examples including fire-sprinkler systems, balers, bridge lifts, centrifuges, cremation ovens, sieves, jacks, industrial filters, and scales, supporting the heterogeneous lens decision.
sources.title: 2022 NAICS definition: 333998 All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing

sources.supports: Provides the broad machinery-manufacturing occupational mix used to proxy wage-weighted AI task exposure.
sources.title: May 2023 National Industry-Specific Occupational Employment and Wage Estimates: NAICS 333000 Machinery Manufacturing

sources.supports: Documents U.S.-manufactured screening and processing equipment, food, pharmaceutical, chemical, powder, and beverage end markets, and aftermarket replacement parts for its own and other equipment.
sources.title: Kason Corporation: Screening, Sifting and Processing Equipment

sources.supports: Documents inspection, repair and rebuild across many centrifuge types and OEM brands, in-house wear-part fabrication, balancing, testing, nationwide field service, and emergency response.
sources.title: Centrifuge World: Industrial Centrifuge Specialists

sources.supports: Shows custom filtration systems, replacement parts, maintenance, servicing, and long equipment lives across industrial and municipal applications.
sources.title: Compositech Filters: Industrial Vacuum Filtration Equipment, Parts and Service

sources.supports: Provides broad manufacturing closed-transaction and valuation benchmarks for 2021-2025, used only as a transfer-probability proxy.
sources.title: BizBuySell Manufacturing Business Valuation Benchmarks

sources.supports: Provides economy-wide owner-age and ownership-characteristics tables used only as a broad succession proxy.
sources.title: 2022 Annual Business Survey: Characteristics of Business Owners

sources.supports: Reports June 2026 industrial production 1.1% above a year earlier, second-quarter manufacturing output growth at a 4.7% annual rate, and below-average manufacturing capacity utilization, supporting a cautious broad demand proxy.
sources.title: Federal Reserve: Industrial Production and Capacity Utilization, July 17 2026

sources.supports: Documents ongoing low-cost federal credit assistance for wastewater, drinking-water, and stormwater projects, a demand-adjacent support for municipal separation and filtration equipment.
sources.title: U.S. EPA Water Infrastructure Finance and Innovation Act Program

title: All Other Miscellaneous General Purpose Machinery Manufacturing
